title: Kengyilia melanthera
intro: Kengyilia melanthera, also known as Tibetan sweet grass or sweet grass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, which can help alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs. The bioactive constituents of Kengyilia melanthera include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional Tibetan medicine, Kengyilia melanthera is often used to make herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, including respiratory issues, fever, and digestive problems.
